openingMessage: In leadership, there are no words more important than trust.
openingWhy: When you write 0 ppm on a form, the next crew opens that line without re-testing — your signature is their gas test. Fudge one reading and every number you've ever signed becomes a question. There's no re-cal for a hand nobody trusts.
leadershipThought: You are not just doing a job. You are building the next generation of people who will do this safely. That is what it means to be an Owl.

fieldNews.headline: Caleb Smith: Crew and MT conducted pretask briefing, crew was thorough with every step of job plan. — Everyone on location performed well on every task.
Alfredo Vaquera: Great communication and work ethic with all personnel — Everyone followed all psms in place
Rolando Flores: There was a little bit of benzene — We all communicated and used LMRAs
Raymundo Solis: Entrance to pad was farmed and had to clear the path
Anthony Scott: Trucker brought incorrect water to kill well.
SM Energy #112 — 6,000 ppm H2S / 2% CO2 (carry-forward from 7/10)
fieldNews.detail: Michael Hutt reported it Friday. Confirm current readings before any entry or first break this week.

fieldNews.headline: Two driving near-misses flagged Friday — stay heads-up on the road
fieldNews.detail: Jordan Lemmons (swerving driver, Jal 128) and Alex Clubb (service truck into his lane at 75 mph). The drive is part of the job.

todaysChallenge: If the numbers say stop today, stop — even if the whole location is waiting on you. That call takes courage once. It builds a reputation forever.
todaysChallengeSubtext: “Courage and confidence are what decision making is all about.” — Mike Krzyzewski
